perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS Secure Transportation and the Company wish to enter into discussions during which it will be necessary for each party (the "Disclosing Party") to disclose to the other (the "Receiving Party") information of a confidential or proprietary nature (irrespective of the form of presentation or communication including, but not limited to, computer software and data, physical objects and samples) provided that any disclosure of Information made in writing shall be marked confidential or with words of similar effect and that any disclosure made orally shall subsequently be reduced to writing by the Disclosing Party and marked confidential or with words of similar effect with a copy sent to the Receiving Party within 21 days of disclosure ("Information"). IT IS AGREED AS FOLLOWS: 1.This Agreement will apply to any disclosure of Information on the Effective Date and for [12] months thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS Secure Transportation and the Company wish to enter into discussions during which it will be necessary for each party (the "Disclosing Entity") to disclose to the other (the "Receiving Party") data of a confidential or proprietary type (irrespective of the form of presentation or communication including, but not limited to, computer software and data, physical objects and samples) provided that any disclosure of Data made in writing shall be marked confidential or with words of similar effect and that any disclosure made orally shall subsequently be reduced to writing by the Disclosing Entity and marked confidential or with words of similar effect with a copy sent to the Receiving Party within 21 days of disclosure ("Information"). IT IS AGREED AS FOLLOWS: 1.This Agreement will apply to any disclosure of Data on the Effective Date and for [12] months thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: The terms "Disclosing Party" and "Disclosing Entity" are used interchangeably without clear definition, potentially leading to confusion about the roles and responsibilities. Furthermore, the interchangeable use of "Information" and "Data" is not defined leading to ambiguity, creating an in-text contradiction as the contract uses the term "Information" which has its own definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS Secure Transportation and the Company wish to enter into discussions during which it will be necessary for each party (the "Disclosing Entity") to disclose to the other (the "Receiving Party") data of a confidential or proprietary type (irrespective of the form of presentation or communication including, but not limited to, computer software and data, physical objects and samples) provided that any disclosure of Data made in writing shall be marked confidential or with words of similar effect and that any disclosure made orally shall subsequently be reduced to writing by the Disclosing Entity and marked confidential or with words of similar effect with a copy sent to the Receiving Party within 21 days of disclosure ("Information"). IT IS AGREED AS FOLLOWS: 1.This Agreement will apply to any disclosure of Data on the Effective Date and for [12] months thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces "Data" without clearly defining it in relation to "Information," while simultaneously using both terms and equating them with parentheses around "Information". This creates ambiguity and contradicts the initial definition of "Information," potentially leading to confusion about which definition applies. Since the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, there is a strong in-text contradiction present. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition provided. The change creates a direct conflict with how 'Information' is used and defined throughout the rest of the document, particularly in the contradicted text which uses the updated term. Therefore the change breaks compatibility.

perturbation.original_text: 2.The Receiving Party shall keep confidential the Information for [five (5)] years from the date of disclosure, and shall not disclose the Information to any other person or company without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent, and shall not itself make any use of such Information for any purpose other than the purpose of supply of logistics services ("the Authorised Purpose").
perturbation.changed_text: 2.The Receiving Party shall keep confidential the Data for [five (5)] years from the date of disclosure, and shall not disclose the Confidential Materials to any other person or company without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent, and shall not itself make any use of such Data for any purpose other than the purpose of supply of logistics services ("the Authorised Purpose").
perturbation.explanation: The terms "Information", "Data", and "Confidential Materials" are used interchangeably without clear definition, potentially leading to confusion. The original text refers to 'Information', while the changed text introduces 'Data' and 'Confidential Materials' creating an in-text contradiction about what is being protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.The Receiving Party shall keep confidential the Data for [five (5)] years from the date of disclosure, and shall not disclose the Confidential Materials to any other person or company without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent, and shall not itself make any use of such Data for any purpose other than the purpose of supply of logistics services ("the Authorised Purpose").
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Information', but the changed text uses 'Data' and 'Confidential Materials' interchangeably, and the contradicted text also uses 'Data' and 'Confidential Materials' while not defining the differences between those terms. Since the definitions don't explain that 'Information' can be replaced with 'Data' and 'Confidential Materials' it creates an in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation type, creating a strong in-text contradiction, therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: 3.The Receiving Party shall: (a) take the same care in protecting the Disclosing Party's Information as it takes in protecting its own confidential information and in any event not less than that which a reasonable person or business would take in protecting its own confidential information; (b) only disclose Information on a need-to-know basis to such of their employees, agents, consultants and contractors as are under similar obligations of confidentiality as contained in this Agreement including, but not limited to, the use of the Information for the Authorised Purpose only; and (c) forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination: (i) return all information supplied by the Disclosing Party as well as items and materials relating to or derived from the Information; (ii) deliver to the Disclosing Party or at its request destroy immediately all items and materials made by the Receiving Party containing Information, that are not returned pursuant to paragraph (i) above; (iii) not keep copies or duplicates of any items or materials referred to in paragraphs (i) or (ii) above; and (iv) provide a certificate signed by a senior officer of the Receiving Party confirming that the provisions of this clause have been complied with.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.The Receiving Party shall: (a) take the same care in protecting the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Data as it takes in protecting its own sensitive information and in any event not less than that which a reasonable person or business would take in protecting its own confidential information; (b) only disclose Confidential Information on a need-to-know basis to such of their employees, agents, consultants and contractors as are under similar obligations of confidentiality as contained in this Agreement including, but not limited to, the use of the Confidential Information for the Authorised Purpose only; and (c) forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination: (i) return all Data supplied by the Disclosing Party as well as items and materials relating to or derived from the Proprietary Data; (ii) deliver to the Disclosing Party or at its request destroy immediately all items and materials made by the Receiving Party containing Confidential Information, that are not returned pursuant to paragraph (i) above; (iii) not keep copies or duplicates of any items or materials referred to in paragraphs (i) or (ii) above; and (iv) provide a certificate signed by a senior officer of the Receiving Party confirming that the provisions of this clause have been complied with.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses "Information", while the changed text introduces "Proprietary Data" and "Confidential Information". These terms are used interchangeably without definition, causing confusion about the exact type of data being protected. This change directly contradicts the originally defined term "Information" creating ambiguity and uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.The Receiving Party shall: (a) take the same care in protecting the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Data as it takes in protecting its own sensitive information and in any event not less than that which a reasonable person or business would take in protecting its own confidential information; (b) only disclose Confidential Information on a need-to-know basis to such of their employees, agents, consultants and contractors as are under similar obligations of confidentiality as contained in this Agreement including, but not limited to, the use of the Confidential Information for the Authorised Purpose only; and (c) forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination: (i) return all Data supplied by the Disclosing Party as well as items and materials relating to or derived from the Proprietary Data; (ii) deliver to the Disclosing Party or at its request destroy immediately all items and materials made by the Receiving Party containing Confidential Information, that are not returned pursuant to paragraph (i) above; (iii) not keep copies or duplicates of any items or materials referred to in paragraphs (i) or (ii) above; and (iv) provide a certificate signed by a senior officer of the Receiving Party confirming that the provisions of this clause have been complied with.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple new terms ('Proprietary Data', 'Confidential Information', 'Data') that are used interchangeably with the original term 'Information', without clear definitions. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding which type of data is being protected, directly contradicting the original intent and causing a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition. The Contradicted text uses all these new terms, showing that they are indeed being used, creating contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.This Agreement shall not apply to any Information which the Receiving Party: (a) can show is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) can show was in its possession prior to the date of disclosure; (c) may subsequently receive from any third party legally in possession of the Information and who was not restricted from disclosing it; (d) can show is independently acquired by the Receiving Party as a result of work carried out by an employee, consultant or contractor of the Receiving Party to whom no disclosure of Information has been made; or (e) is required to disclose pursuant to a court order or relevant stock exchange provided that the Receiving Party shall have given prior written notice to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.This Agreement shall not apply to any Sensitive Information which the Receiving Party: (a) can show is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) can show was in its possession prior to the date of disclosure; (c) may subsequently receive from any third party legally in possession of the Protected Data and who was not restricted from disclosing it; (d) can show is independently acquired by the Receiving Party as a result of work carried out by an employee, consultant or contractor of the Receiving Party to whom no disclosure of Company Secrets has been made; or (e) is required to disclose pursuant to a court order or relevant stock exchange provided that the Receiving Party shall have given prior written notice to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Using the terms "Sensitive Information", "Protected Data", and "Company Secrets" interchangeably without defining them creates ambiguity. In the original agreement "Information" had a specific meaning, but now the introduction of these terms causes confusion about which data the exclusions apply to. This introduces an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.This Agreement shall not apply to any Sensitive Information which the Receiving Party: (a) can show is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) can show was in its possession prior to the date of disclosure; (c) may subsequently receive from any third party legally in possession of the Protected Data and who was not restricted from disclosing it; (d) can show is independently acquired by the Receiving Party as a result of work carried out by an employee, consultant or contractor of the Receiving Party to whom no disclosure of Company Secrets has been made; or (e) is required to disclose pursuant to a court order or relevant stock exchange provided that the Receiving Party shall have given prior written notice to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the changed text introduces the terms "Sensitive Information", "Protected Data", and "Company Secrets" interchangeably without defining them, which creates ambiguity. The original agreement used "Information" with a specific meaning, and now the introduction of these terms creates confusion about which data the exclusions apply to, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which reinforces the contradiction because it exhibits the issue that it suffers from this misalignment of terminology. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5.This Agreement shall not be deemed to confer or imply the grant or agreement to grant by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party of any of its rights under copyright, patents, trademarks, design rights or other similar rights. AH Information supplied hereunder is supplied on an "as is" basis and the Disclosing Party gives no representation or warranty as to its accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose. The Disclosing Party shall not be liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Receiving Party as a result of the Receiving Party's use of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.This Agreement shall not be deemed to confer or imply the grant or agreement to grant by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party of any of its rights under copyright, patents, trademarks, design rights or other similar rights. AH Company Information supplied hereunder is supplied on an "as is" basis and the Disclosing Party gives no representation or warranty as to its accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose. The Disclosing Party shall not be liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Receiving Party as a result of the Receiving Party's use of the Confidential Data.
perturbation.explanation: The terms "Information", "Company Information", and "Confidential Data" are used inconsistently. Originally "Information" was the key defined term. Now using "Company Information" and "Confidential Data" creates an internal contradiction, since the parties can now claim that some information they share isn't covered by the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.This Agreement shall not be deemed to confer or imply the grant or agreement to grant by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party of any of its rights under copyright, patents, trademarks, design rights or other similar rights. AH Company Information supplied hereunder is supplied on an "as is" basis and the Disclosing Party gives no representation or warranty as to its accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose. The Disclosing Party shall not be liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Receiving Party as a result of the Receiving Party's use of the Confidential Data.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the terms 'Company Information' and 'Confidential Data' where the original text used the term 'Information'. This creates a contradiction because it's no longer clear which information is covered by the agreement. The contradicted text uses the same terms, thus creating an in-text contradiction by using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, which falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding relative to the protection of the Information covered by this Agreement, is executed by authorised representatives of each party and supersedes all prior and collateral communications, reports and understandings, if any, between the parties regarding the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding relative to the protection of the Intellectual Property covered by this Agreement, is executed by authorised representatives of each party and supersedes all prior and collateral communications, reports and understandings, if any, between the parties regarding the Protected Information.
perturbation.explanation: The terms "Information", "Intellectual Property" and "Protected Information" are used interchangeably without definition and creates an in-text contradiction. This weakens the entire agreement clause because it is not clear what information the agreement seeks to protect. Originally it was only Information now using Intellectual Property can include things such as copyright, trademarks, or patents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding relative to the protection of the Intellectual Property covered by this Agreement, is executed by authorised representatives of each party and supersedes all prior and collateral communications, reports and understandings, if any, between the parties regarding the Protected Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the term "Information" with "Intellectual Property" and "Protected Information" without defining these terms, which creates a contradiction. The explanation states that this interchangeable use of terms weakens the agreement because it's unclear what the agreement protects. Originally it was just 'Information' and now using 'Intellectual Property' can include things such as copyright, trademarks, or patents. This directly leads to ambiguity and an in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, indicating that this contradiction exists within the same clause.
